prerequisites: A Square account with access to the Square Developer Console (developer.squareup.com).
A Square application created in the Developer Console, which provides per-environment credentials (production and sandbox).
Decide which environment to read from and set the is_sandbox config field accordingly: sandbox uses connect.squareupsandbox.com, production uses connect.squareup.com.
Optionally set start_date to bound the earliest records pulled, and include_deleted_objects if you want deleted objects returned where the API supports it.

authMethods.name: Personal access token (credentials.api_key)
authMethods.summary: A personal access token grants unrestricted Square API access to resources in your own Square account, suitable for connecting a single account. In the Developer Console, open your application, go to the Credentials section, select the Production or Sandbox environment, and copy the access token into the credentials.api_key field. Sandbox and production each have their own token, so it must match the is_sandbox setting.

authMethods.name: OAuth (credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, credentials.refresh_token)
authMethods.summary: OAuth provides scoped access to a seller's account and is used when connecting accounts you do not own. Supply your application's Application ID as client_id and Application Secret as client_secret (both found in the Developer Console Credentials section). Run Square's OAuth code flow to exchange an authorization code at the ObtainToken endpoint for an access token and a refresh token, then provide that refresh_token. Access tokens expire after 30 days; the connector uses the long-lived refresh token to obtain new access tokens.

setupSteps.title: Create or open a Square application
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Square Developer Console and create an application (or open an existing one). The application exposes separate credentials for the Production and Sandbox environments.

setupSteps.title: Choose the environment
setupSteps.body: Set the required is_sandbox config field to match the environment whose data you want. Sandbox calls go to connect.squareupsandbox.com and production calls go to connect.squareup.com; using a token from the wrong environment causes authentication errors.

setupSteps.title: Authenticate with a personal access token
setupSteps.body: For a single, self-owned account, open the application's Credentials section, select Production or Sandbox, reveal the access token, and put it in credentials.api_key. Ensure it matches the is_sandbox setting.

setupSteps.title: Or authenticate with OAuth
setupSteps.body: To connect a seller account via OAuth, set credentials.client_id to your Application ID and credentials.client_secret to your Application Secret. Complete Square's OAuth authorization flow to obtain a refresh token from the ObtainToken endpoint and set credentials.refresh_token. Square uses the refresh token to mint fresh access tokens as the 30-day access tokens expire.

setupSteps.title: Set optional sync controls
setupSteps.body: Optionally set start_date to limit how far back records are read, and enable include_deleted_objects to include deleted objects for endpoints that support returning them.
